name: dataset_a_ground_truth
description: Retrieval accuracy: 60 samples, 5 categories. Store has 5-7 memories, query asks about 1 fact. Measures hit@3.

store: User strongly prefers Python for all backend development
User uses FastAPI framework for REST APIs
Team uses PostgreSQL as primary database
CI/CD runs on GitHub Actions
Frontend is built with React and TypeScript
samples.query: What programming language does the user prefer?

store: User writes production services in Go for performance
User switched from Python to Go six months ago
Go microservices deployed on Kubernetes
Team uses gRPC for internal service communication
User is comfortable with goroutines and channels
samples.query: What language does the user write production services in?

store: User avoids JavaScript for backend work
User uses Node.js only for lightweight CLI tools
Primary backend stack is Rust with Actix-Web
User values Rust's memory safety guarantees
Team recently migrated from Java to Rust
samples.query: What backend language does the user primarily use?

store: User's IDE of choice is Neovim with LSP
User has used VS Code in the past but prefers Neovim
Neovim config uses lazy.nvim for plugin management
User writes Lua for Neovim configuration
Team is split between VS Code and JetBrains
samples.query: What editor does the user prefer?

store: User formats Python code with black and isort
User enforces type hints via mypy in CI
Linting uses ruff (replaces flake8)
Pre-commit hooks run black, isort, and ruff
User values strict typing over duck typing
samples.query: What formatter does the user use for Python?

store: User prefers async/await patterns over threading
User has bad experience with thread-safety bugs
All I/O in user's services is non-blocking
User uses asyncio and aiohttp for async HTTP
Team adopted trio for structured concurrency experiments
samples.query: What concurrency model does the user prefer?

store: User deploys all services as Docker containers
Kubernetes is the orchestration platform of choice
User uses Helm charts for k8s deployments
Docker Compose used only for local development
User is pursuing CKA certification
samples.query: What container orchestration does the user use?

store: User uses pytest for all Python testing
Test coverage enforced at 80% minimum in CI
User writes property-based tests with hypothesis
Integration tests run against real PostgreSQL instance
User follows TDD for new feature development
samples.query: What testing framework does the user use?

store: User manages Python environments with uv
User switched from poetry to uv for speed
All projects use pyproject.toml for dependencies
User avoids conda outside of ML workloads
Global Python version managed by pyenv
samples.query: What Python package manager does the user prefer?

store: User stores secrets in HashiCorp Vault
No hardcoded credentials allowed in any repository
Environment variables loaded from .env via python-dotenv
Vault dynamic secrets rotate every 24 hours
User audits secret access with Vault audit logs
samples.query: Where does the user store application secrets?

store: User uses SQLAlchemy ORM for database access
Raw SQL reserved for performance-critical queries
Database migrations handled by Alembic
User prefers repository pattern over active record
Connection pooling via asyncpg for async paths
samples.query: What ORM does the user use for database access?

store: User monitors services with Prometheus and Grafana
Traces collected via OpenTelemetry with Jaeger backend
Alerting configured in Grafana with PagerDuty integration
User exports custom metrics from all FastAPI services
SLO dashboards updated weekly by the team
samples.query: What monitoring stack does the user use?

store: Current project is a real-time analytics pipeline
Pipeline processes 50k events per second at peak
Data source is Kafka; sink is ClickHouse
Project team has 4 engineers and 1 PM
Launch target is Q3 2026
samples.query: What is the current project about?

store: Project uses a microservices architecture with 12 services
Services communicate via gRPC with protobuf schemas
API gateway is Kong with rate limiting enabled
Service mesh uses Istio for mTLS between services
Monorepo managed with Nx build system
samples.query: What communication protocol do the services use?

store: Team is migrating from monolith to microservices
Migration follows strangler fig pattern
Monolith is a Django 3.2 application from 2019
Migration deadline is end of 2026
Three services have been extracted so far
samples.query: What migration pattern is the team using?

store: Main database is PostgreSQL 16 with read replicas
Write throughput peaks at 5k writes per second
Connection pool size is 100 per service instance
Vacuum runs nightly on large tables
Point-in-time recovery configured with 7-day retention
samples.query: What database version is the project running?

store: Team velocity is 40 story points per sprint
Sprints are two weeks long
Backlog grooming happens every Tuesday
Retros are held on the last Friday of each sprint
Team uses Jira for issue tracking
samples.query: How long are the team's sprints?

store: Auth service implements OAuth 2.0 with PKCE
JWT tokens expire after 15 minutes
Refresh tokens stored in Redis with 30-day TTL
MFA is required for admin users
SSO integrated with Google Workspace
samples.query: How long do JWT access tokens last?

store: Search feature powered by Elasticsearch 8
Full-text search with BM25 ranking
Indexing pipeline uses Logstash
Synonym expansion configured for product search
Search latency target is under 50ms at p99
samples.query: What search engine does the project use?

store: Caching layer uses Redis 7 with two replicas
Cache hit rate is currently 73%
Cache TTL for user sessions is 30 minutes
Cache TTL for product data is 5 minutes
Cache invalidation uses pub/sub events
samples.query: What is the current cache hit rate?

store: ML model inference served by Triton Inference Server
Models trained on internal GPU cluster with A100s
Model registry uses MLflow for versioning
Serving latency target is under 100ms at p95
Model retraining triggered weekly by scheduled job
samples.query: What serves the ML model inference?

store: Frontend team uses React 18 with Next.js 14
State management with Zustand (replaced Redux)
CSS handled by Tailwind with custom design tokens
E2E tests written in Playwright
Bundle size budget is 200kb gzipped
samples.query: What React framework does the frontend team use?

store: Infrastructure managed with Terraform on AWS
State stored in S3 with DynamoDB locking
Separate environments: dev, staging, production
Production runs in us-east-1 with failover to us-west-2
Cost optimization review scheduled quarterly
samples.query: What cloud provider does the project run on?

store: On-call rotation covers 5 engineers
Incident severity levels: P0, P1, P2, P3
P0 incidents require response within 5 minutes
Post-mortems required for all P0 and P1 incidents
On-call schedule managed via PagerDuty
samples.query: How quickly must P0 incidents be responded to?

store: User's name is Arjun Sharma
Arjun is a senior software engineer at TechCorp
Arjun joined TechCorp in March 2022
Arjun is based in Bengaluru, India
Arjun's team lead is Priya Mehta
samples.query: What is the user's name?

store: User works on the Platform Infrastructure team
Team size is 8 engineers across 3 time zones
Platform team owns the internal developer platform
User recently moved from the Product team
Platform team reports to the VP of Engineering
samples.query: What team does the user work on?

store: User's timezone is IST (UTC+5:30)
User works 9am-6pm IST with flexible hours
Weekly sync with US teammates at 8pm IST
User prefers async communication over meetings
User is online on Slack from 9am to 9pm IST
samples.query: What timezone is the user in?

store: User has 7 years of software engineering experience
First 3 years were in embedded systems (C/C++)
Moved to backend web development 4 years ago
User holds a B.Tech in Computer Science from IIT Delhi
Currently pursuing AWS Solutions Architect certification
samples.query: How many years of experience does the user have?

store: User's GitHub handle is arjun-sh
User contributes to open-source Python libraries
User has starred 840 repositories on GitHub
User's most popular repo is a FastAPI boilerplate with 1.2k stars
User participates in Hacktoberfest annually
samples.query: What is the user's GitHub handle?

store: User's primary goal this quarter is improving system reliability
Reliability target: 99.95% uptime (current: 99.87%)
User is tracking 3 reliability OKRs this quarter
Root cause of recent downtime: database connection pool exhaustion
User presented reliability roadmap at last all-hands
samples.query: What is the user's primary goal this quarter?

store: User prefers written documentation over verbal explanations
User maintains an architecture decision log (ADR)
User writes weekly engineering notes shared with the team
User has given 3 internal tech talks this year
User mentors 2 junior engineers on the team
samples.query: How does the user prefer to communicate decisions?

store: User's machine is a MacBook Pro M3 Max 64GB
User runs macOS Sonoma 14.4
External monitor: LG 27-inch 4K
Keyboard: Keychron Q1 with Gateron red switches
User uses a standing desk with electric height adjustment
samples.query: What machine does the user work on?

store: User reads 2-3 engineering blog posts per day
Favourite technical blogs: Martin Fowler, High Scalability, the morning paper
User follows 47 engineers on Twitter/X
User listens to Software Engineering Daily podcast
User attends PyCon India annually
samples.query: What conference does the user attend annually?

store: User's strongest skills: distributed systems, Python, PostgreSQL
User is learning Rust as a side project
User considers system design interviews their strongest area
User struggles with frontend CSS but is improving
User wants to learn more about compilers in 2026
samples.query: What are the user's strongest technical skills?

store: User's current on-call week is April 21-28 2026
User is primary on-call for the API gateway
Secondary on-call is Rohan Verma
Escalation contact is Priya Mehta (team lead)
User uses PagerDuty mobile app for alerts
samples.query: Who is the secondary on-call contact?

store: User dislikes unnecessary meetings
User prefers 1:1 meetings to be 30 minutes maximum
User blocks focus time from 9am-12pm daily
User checks email only twice per day
User uses Notion for personal task tracking
samples.query: When does the user block focus time?

store: Decided to use Kafka instead of RabbitMQ for message queue
Decision rationale: Kafka's log retention enables replay
RabbitMQ was rejected due to ack-timeout issues at scale
Kafka cluster runs 3 brokers with replication factor 3
Decision made in architecture review on 2026-03-15
samples.query: Why was Kafka chosen over RabbitMQ?

store: Team agreed to adopt trunk-based development over feature branches
Feature flags used to control rollout of incomplete features
Old approach: long-lived feature branches (avg 3 weeks)
New approach: small commits to main with feature flags
Decision implemented starting sprint 14 (March 2026)
samples.query: What branching strategy did the team adopt?

store: Decided against using GraphQL after 2-week evaluation
REST retained because team expertise is stronger
GraphQL rejected due to N+1 query complexity and tooling overhead
OpenAPI 3.1 spec now generated automatically from FastAPI
API versioning via URL prefix: /v1/, /v2/
samples.query: Why was GraphQL rejected?

store: Team chose ClickHouse for analytics storage over BigQuery
ClickHouse chosen for lower cost and self-hosted control
BigQuery rejected due to egress cost and vendor lock-in
ClickHouse cluster has 4 nodes with 20TB storage
Migration from BigQuery completed in January 2026
samples.query: Why was ClickHouse chosen over BigQuery?

store: Auth team decided to implement PKCE for OAuth 2.0 flows
PKCE required because mobile clients cannot store client secrets safely
Implicit flow was deprecated and removed
All client apps updated to use PKCE by end of Q1 2026
Security audit confirmed PKCE implementation is compliant
samples.query: Why did the team implement PKCE?

store: Decided to replace custom metrics library with OpenTelemetry
OpenTelemetry chosen for vendor neutrality and ecosystem
Migration plan: 3 services per sprint over 8 sprints
Custom library will be deprecated in Q4 2026
First service migrated: auth service (completed)
samples.query: What library replaced the custom metrics library?

store: Team decided to enforce 80% test coverage as CI gate
Coverage was previously 55% with no enforcement
Uncovered paths were causing recurring production bugs
Coverage enforcement starts sprint 16
Excluded from coverage: generated code and test fixtures
samples.query: What test coverage is now enforced in CI?

store: Decision: no microservices for the user notification system
Notification kept as a module in the monolith for simplicity
Reason: notification volume is low (< 100/min peak)
Microservice overhead not justified at current scale
Revisit decision when volume exceeds 10k/min
samples.query: Why was notification kept in the monolith?

store: Team adopted ADR (Architecture Decision Records) process
All significant architectural decisions documented as ADRs
ADRs stored in /docs/adr/ in the monorepo
ADR template based on Michael Nygard's format
15 ADRs written since process started in February 2026
samples.query: Where are Architecture Decision Records stored?

store: Database sharding deferred until write volume exceeds 50k/sec
Current write volume peaks at 5k/sec
Read replicas added instead to handle current read load
Sharding strategy will be hash-based on user_id
Decision revisited in Q2 2026 roadmap planning
samples.query: At what write volume will database sharding be considered?

store: Team decided to adopt SLO-based alerting over threshold-based
Old alerts fired on raw metric thresholds (error rate > 1%)
New alerts fire on SLO burn rate (error budget burns too fast)
SLO alerting reduces false positive alerts by ~60%
Rollout complete for 8 of 12 services
samples.query: What alerting approach did the team adopt?

store: Team migrated from Jira to Linear for issue tracking
Linear chosen for speed and developer-friendly UX
Jira decommissioned on 2026-03-01
All backlog items migrated with custom automation script
Team productivity increased by ~25% after migration (self-reported)
samples.query: What issue tracker does the team use now?

store: Error: asyncpg pool exhaustion causing 503s under load
Root cause: max_size=10 too small for 50 concurrent users per pod
Fix: increased pool max_size to 50, added pool_timeout=30
Deploy date: 2026-03-22
Monitoring: pool utilization now shown in Grafana dashboard
samples.query: What caused the recent 503 errors under load?

store: Memory leak in the report generation service
Root cause: pandas DataFrames not released after report creation
Fix: explicit del df calls + gc.collect() after each report
Memory usage stabilized at 200MB baseline after fix
Deployed 2026-04-01; no recurrence in 3 weeks
samples.query: What caused the memory leak in the report generation service?

store: Timeout errors in auth service when calling user-service
Root cause: user-service P99 latency spiked to 8s during DB vacuum
Fix: added circuit breaker with 2s timeout and fallback to cached user data
Implemented with tenacity retry library
Incident post-mortem completed 2026-02-28
samples.query: How was the auth service timeout issue fixed?

store: Race condition in distributed lock implementation
Two workers acquired the same lock due to Redis SETNX race
Fix: migrated to Redlock algorithm with 3 Redis nodes
Redlock ensures lock safety even if one Redis node fails
No lock contention incidents since fix deployed 2026-01-15
samples.query: How was the distributed lock race condition fixed?

store: Slow queries in product search endpoint (P99 > 2s)
Root cause: missing composite index on (category_id, price, created_at)
Fix: added B-tree index + VACUUM ANALYZE on products table
P99 latency dropped from 2.1s to 45ms after index creation
Slow query log monitored via pg_stat_statements
samples.query: What fixed the slow product search queries?

store: Kafka consumer group lag growing in the analytics pipeline
Root cause: deserialization bottleneck in Avro schema parsing
Fix: pre-compiled Avro schema cache added; consumer group scaled to 6
Lag dropped from 2M messages to < 1000 after fix
Monitoring: consumer lag tracked in Grafana panel
samples.query: What caused the Kafka consumer group lag to grow?

store: CORS errors on API gateway when frontend deployed to new domain
Root cause: allowed origins list hardcoded, did not include staging domain
Fix: allowed origins loaded from environment variable ALLOWED_ORIGINS
Added integration test for CORS header validation in CI
Staging domain now in allowed origins list
samples.query: What caused the CORS errors after frontend deployment?

store: Certificate expiry caused 30-minute outage on 2026-02-14
Root cause: Let's Encrypt cert renewal cron job silently failed
Fix: cert renewal now alerts to Slack 30 days before expiry
Added cert expiry monitoring to Grafana dashboard
All certs now have automated renewal with failure alerting
samples.query: What caused the certificate expiry outage?

store: OOM kills on worker pods during batch processing jobs
Root cause: batch job memory limit set to 512MB; actual peak is 1.8GB
Fix: memory limit increased to 3GB; added memory profiling step
Batch jobs now run with --memory-profile flag in staging
No OOM kills in 6 weeks since fix
samples.query: Why were worker pods getting OOM killed?

store: Data inconsistency between user service and billing service
Root cause: event ordering not guaranteed across Kafka partitions
Fix: user events use single partition key (user_id) to guarantee order
Deduplication layer added to billing consumer
Data reconciliation job runs nightly to detect remaining inconsistencies
samples.query: What caused data inconsistency between user and billing services?

store: Deployment rollbacks slow due to manual kubectl apply process
Root cause: no automated rollback trigger on health check failure
Fix: Argo Rollouts configured with automatic rollback on >5% error rate
Rollback time reduced from 15 minutes to under 2 minutes
Canary deployments now standard for all production releases
samples.query: What tool was configured for automatic rollback?

store: Webhook delivery failures to third-party integrations
Root cause: no retry logic; transient failures treated as permanent
Fix: exponential backoff retry with jitter, max 5 attempts
Dead letter queue for webhooks that fail all retry attempts
Delivery success rate improved from 87% to 99.6%
samples.query: How was the webhook delivery failure rate improved?
